title: pedicularis rhaetica var. letourneuxii
intro: Pedicularis rhaetica var. letourneuxii, also known as Alpine Lousewort, is a rare, flowering plant species that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pedicularis rhaetica var. letourneuxii include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water, making it suitable for teas and infusions.
